data.title: New Mexico Senator Submits Bitcoin Strategic Reserve Bill, Proposing to Allocate 5% of Public Funds
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News U.S. Senator Ant Thornton submitted the "Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Act" to the New Mexico Senate, proposing to invest 5% of public funds in Bitcoin, aiming to establish an alternative treasury for the state. The proposed 5% allocation will diversify the state's funds and enable it to benefit from the long-term price appreciation of Bitcoin.
The bill also proposes that the Bitcoin reserve be managed by the state investment officer and operate under the supervision of the state investment committee. The bill also clarifies the need for cold storage as a viable means of protecting the state's Bitcoin.

data.title: New Mexico Senator Proposes Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Bill
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Senator Ant Thornton has introduced the 'Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Bill' to the New Mexico Senate. The proposal suggests investing 5% of public funds in Bitcoin to establish an alternative treasury for the state. This allocation aims to diversify the state's funds and capitalize on Bitcoin's potential long-term price appreciation. The bill also recommends that the state's investment officer manage the Bitcoin reserve under the oversight of the State Investment Council. Additionally, it emphasizes the importance of using cold storage as a viable method to safeguard the state's Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: Phantom Wallet's January revenue exceeded $29 million, a record high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: DeFiLlama data shows that Phantom Wallet's in-app exchange fees exceeded US$29 million in January, setting a record high for monthly revenue.

data.title: Michael Saylor Teases “Big Strategy Day,” Crypto Community Reacts
data.description:
Michael Saylor’s cryptic social media post has ignited speculation following MicroStrategy’s pause in Bitcoin acquisitions. With potential shifts in corporate strategy and regulatory engagement, investors are closely watching his next move.
The post Michael Saylor Teases “Big Strategy Day,” Crypto Community Reacts appeared first on Cryptonews.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/michael-saylor-teases-big-strategy-day-crypto-community-reacts/

data.title: BlackRock To List New Spot Bitcoin ETP in Europe Following Big Success of US Product: Report
data.description:
Asset management titan BlackRock is reportedly going to list a new spot market Bitcoin (BTC) exchange-traded product (ETP) in Europe, its first digital asset-related venture into the continent. According to a new report from Bloomberg, BlackRock – which has over $10 trillion in assets under management – is getting ready to launch a BTC ETP […]

data.title: Musk supports putting US Treasury transactions on-chain
data.description: According to Watcher.Guru on the X platform, Musk supports putting U.S. Treasury transactions on the blockchain for full transparency.

data.title: Elon Musk Advocates For Blockchain Transparency In U.S. Treasury Transactions
data.description: According to Odaily, Elon Musk has expressed support for placing U.S. Treasury transactions on the blockchain to achieve complete transparency. This statement was shared by Watcher.Guru on the X platform, highlighting Musk's advocacy for leveraging blockchain technology to enhance financial transparency.

data.title: Strategy reports GAAP loss per share of $3.03 in Q4 2024
data.description: Bitcoin Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) reported a net loss of $3.03 per share in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared with earnings of $0.50 per share in the same period last year. The loss was caused by the company's impairment charge on its Bitcoin holdings. As of December 31, 2024, the Bitcoin holdings were 447,470, while the market expected 425,149. The company recently ended a series of Bitcoin purchases and currently holds 471,107 Bitcoins, worth about $45 billion. The U.S. Financial Stability Accounting Board (FASB) implemented a new fair value accounting rule for companies holding digital assets last year. The use of the rule is voluntary before the end of 2024, but it will be mandatory starting in the first quarter of this year.
